mnemonics.title: SRAM vs DRAM: 'S for Speed, D for Dense'
mnemonics.content: SRAM = Speed (fast, 6 transistors, no refresh, expensive, cache). DRAM = Dense (1 transistor + capacitor, needs refresh, cheap, main memory). S is fast but costly; D is dense but slow.

mnemonics.title: Memory Hierarchy: 'R-C-M-S'
mnemonics.content: Registers -> Cache -> Main Memory -> Secondary Storage. 'Really Cool Memory Systems' — each level is slower but bigger and cheaper. Speed decreases and size increases as you go down.

mnemonics.title: Locality: 'Time and Space'
mnemonics.content: Temporal = Time (same data accessed again soon). Spatial = Space (nearby data accessed soon). Together they ensure the cache holds what the CPU needs ~90-98% of the time.
